issue_title: Make style presets preview real episode layouts
issue_body:
**Phase:** Perfect — polishing the core experience

Replace the tiny abstract preset thumbnails with large, convincing previews that show how a polished podcast episode will actually look for each style, using realistic speaker frames, captions, title treatment, overlays, and pacing cues. Keep Blank show visually secondary so creators are guided toward strong preset defaults before choosing a custom path.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
In the running new-show setup, the preset picker shows large rich preview cards for every named preset, each visibly distinct and representative of a publishable podcast layout. Selecting a preset updates a larger preview area that clearly shows the chosen episode look on sample multi-speaker content, including speaker framing, captions or title treatment, and overlays where relevant. The selected preset remains highlighted, the show name remains preserved while switching presets, Blank show is secondary to the presets, and `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The product is past MVP on paper, but the maintainer’s rendered UI assessment shows the core preset-first experience is still not demo-quality. The central promise is that creators can choose a professional style and preview the result without manual layout work; right now they are choosing from placeholder-like boxes. Polishing this core decision point is the highest-leverage next step before any further expansion._
